content: Absence/unavailability in scheduling grids — observed in Deputy and When I Work: (1) Approved leave = solid colored cell (Deputy: red; WhenIWork: grey). (2) Pending leave request = dotted/striped cell in the same color. (3) Recurring unavailability (day-of-week rule) = distinct color (Deputy: purple, WhenIWork: grey bar). (4) Scheduling conflict with absent/unavailable day = red flag/indicator on the shift cell. (5) Auto-block: approved leave typically prevents shift recommendations in that slot; manager sees warning when manually overriding. (6) Table stakes: approved absence dates are visually distinct from available days in the grid. Absence-as-unavailability (proactive discourage) is the industry norm — showing only a booking-time warning without proactive grid marking is below the norm. Sources: WhenIWork help, Deputy leave management docs.
